input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: When the question requests whether an artist is/was a member of a band (e.g., asks for a 'former member of the Eagles') AND the supplied passages attribute the work to a named artist (an extracted artist string {ARTIST}) but none of the passages contain an explicit, literal statement that {ARTIST} is/was a member of the asked band.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the original output format and every required behavior of the base prompt: begin with [[ ## reasoning ## ]], include per-passage quote/paraphrase → ENTITY/SCOPE mappings, evaluate whether the exact supporting text for the required fact is present, follow the 'if missing' retrieval-plan structure, and finish with [[ ## summary ## ]]. Do not remove or alter existing mapping, citation, or refusal rules.
delta_p.change_rule:
After performing the mandated per-passage mappings, if no passage contains an explicit membership statement linking the credited artist to the band, append exactly one concise first-hop status sentence and one explicit missing-fact / retrieval-direction block as follows (use the artist name string discovered in the passages for {ARTIST}):
1) First-hop status sentence (one line): concisely state that the retrieved song page attributes the work to {ARTIST} (e.g., 'First-hop: passages attribute the work to {ARTIST}').
2) Bridge / missing fact sentence (one line): state the single missing fact required to answer the question, in this exact template: 'Bridge / missing fact: whether {ARTIST} is (or was) a member of the [asked band].'
3) Retrieval direction (short block): name the canonical retrieval target as the artist (canonical/title-first form = {ARTIST}) and produce a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan per the base prompt: at least three short query variants (ordered by priority) where the first begins with '{ARTIST}' and other variants add disambiguating scope/context tokens (examples: 'band membership', 'Eagles membership', 'biography', 'years'). Also specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer, using a literal-pattern formulation such as: "Literal supporting-text required: '{ARTIST}' with an explicit phrase 'member of the Eagles' or 'was a member of the Eagles' or 'lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles' (optionally with membership years)."
4) Provide the required brief bridging-phrases to include in the follow-up query (one- or two-token connectors) such as '{ARTIST}' + 'Eagles' or '{ARTIST}' + 'band membership'.
Do not produce a final answer or claim membership here; only emit the above status + missing-fact + retrieval-direction when membership is not literally present in the passages. If an explicit membership sentence is present, follow the original 'final-answer extraction' behavior instead.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or invent the final answer when membership is not literally present. Do not output a completed final answer, years of membership, or any new factual claims that are not directly quoted from the passages. Avoid long verbatim spans beyond what the base mapping requires. Do not alter the required section labels, and do not replace the mandated retrieval-plan structure—only add the concise status and retrieval-direction block described above.
delta_p.output_contract: When triggered, the agent's reasoning block must include: (a) the usual per-passage mappings, (b) a one-line first-hop status noting which artist the passages attribute the song to (using the actual name string from the passages), (c) a one-line 'Bridge / missing fact' exactly as templated naming the missing membership fact, and (d) a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan anchored on the canonical artist name ({ARTIST}) including at least three short query variants (first begins with '{ARTIST}'), the literal supporting-text pattern required, and the bridging-phrases to use. The summary section must be either the extracted answer with passage indices (only if the membership statement is present) or a one-line retrieval directive 'retrieve {ARTIST}' (optionally with the top query variant) if membership is missing. All other base-prompt output constraints remain enforced.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This small, targeted rule forces the agent to expose the exact bridge (the artist's band-membership status) and to emit a concrete next-hop retrieval target and prioritized queries when passages attribute the song to an artist but do not state band membership. It converts the current terse single-line output into the required intermediate first-hop summary + retrieval direction without changing other mapping or formatting behavior.

delta_p.trigger: When the reasoning/mapping step identifies a bridge entity whose membership, role, or affiliation with a group (e.g., a person vs. a band) is the missing fact needed to answer the question (for example, passages mention an artist and the band but do not contain an explicit sentence stating membership).
delta_p.preserve_rule: Preserve the existing behavior and formatting rules exactly: keep the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] and [[ ## summary ## ]] blocks; for each passage produce a concise quoted/paraphrased mapping that links quoted text to ENTITY and SCOPE; state whether the exact target fact is present; if present, extract the final answer verbatim with the supporting-text pattern and passage index(es); if absent, emit a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan. Always include passage indices in reasoning.
delta_p.change_rule: When the missing fact is a group-membership or band-affiliation question, augment the 'If missing' retrieval-plan requirements as follows: (a) Name the canonical/title-first target entity to retrieve (e.g., the person's biography page) and give a one-sentence rationale linking that target to the question, citing the bridging passage index. (b) Provide at least three short search-query variants ordered by priority; the first variant MUST begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion (e.g., 'Don Henley biography'), and each variant must include concise scope/context tokens (examples: 'membership', 'Eagles', 'former member', 'lead vocalist', 'biography'). (c) Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer — a literal pattern that the retrieved text must contain (for membership questions, examples of acceptable literal patterns: 'Don Henley is a member of the Eagles', 'Don Henley was a founding member of the Eagles', or 'Don Henley, lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles'). (d) Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect the current passages to the target (e.g., the bridge entity name plus the band name: 'Don Henley' + 'Eagles'). (e) Include a one- or two-sentence short reasoning subsection that cites which passage indices show why a second hop is needed. Do not treat any of these retrieval-plan items as the final answer.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not (a) treat song authorship/credit or album association alone as proof of band membership, (b) invent or assert membership if the explicit supporting-text pattern is absent from provided passages, or (c) use outside memory. Avoid returning the retrieved-target page content as the final answer within this agent; instead require the exact supporting-text pattern be present before extraction.
delta_p.output_contract: When this delta triggers, the agent's 'If missing' block must include: (1) Canonical retrieval target (title-first) and one-sentence rationale citing the bridging passage index; (2) At least three ordered query variants (first begins with the canonical/title-first form) containing scope tokens; (3) The exact literal supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that would permit final extraction (quoted); (4) Exact bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up query; (5) A short reasoning subsection citing passage indices explaining why a second hop is needed. The rest of the summary output must continue to follow the base prompt's formatting and mapping rules, and must not assert the final answer unless the required literal supporting-text pattern is found in the provided passages.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The only missing capability at this step is a precise, canonical-first retrieval instruction for verifying membership/affiliation when a bridge entity is found. This adds a narrowly scoped retrieval-plan requirement (canonical target, ordered queries, literal supporting-text pattern, bridging phrases) without changing existing passage-mapping, formatting, or extraction behavior.

visible_agent_inputs.question: The End of the Innocence was a single by which former member of the Eagles?
passages: The End of the Innocence (song) | "The End of the Innocence" is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album of the same name, released in 1989.  Henley co-wrote and co-produced the song with Bruce Hornsby, who also performed piano; both artists perform the song live in their respective concerts.  Henley's version peaked at number eight on the "Billboard" Hot 100, becoming his fifth solo top ten hit on the chart, more than any of the other members of The Eagles.  "The End of the Innocence" also became his fourth number-one single on the Mainstream Rock Tracks chart and peaked at number two on the Hot Adult Contemporary chart.  The song features Wayne Shorter on saxophone.

The End of the Innocence (album) | The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles.  The album was released in 1989, on Geffen Records, and would be his last release on that label.  It was also his last solo album of the 1980s and it would be eleven years before he released another solo album, until 2000's "Inside Job".

Innocent (Stereophonics song) | "Innocent" is a single by British rock band Stereophonics which was released on 9 November 2009.  It was the first single taken from their seventh studio album, "Keep Calm and Carry On", which was released a week later, on 16 November 2009.  It was also the band's first single for Mercury Records.

Sweet and Innocent (Diamond Head song) | Sweet and Innocent is a single by heavy metal band Diamond Head released in 1980 by Media Records, a Wolverhampton-based label.  It was a single A-side "Streets of Gold" as the B-Side, and was only available on 7".  Both tracks eventually ended up on the re-released version of Diamond Head's 1980 debut Lightning to the Nations in 2001 by Sanctuary Records.

Moving Pictures (band) | Moving Pictures were an Australian rock music band formed in 1978.  Their debut album, "Days of Innocence", was issued in October 1981 and eventually peaked at No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Albums Chart in February the following year.  In January 1982 they released their single, "What About Me", which reached No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Singles Chart.  Late that year Elektra Records issued "Days of Innocence" and "What About Me" in North America.  The single reached No. 29 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and appeared on the associated year-end Hot 100 list for 1983.  A proposed series of United States performances supporting REO Speedwagon, Tom Petty and Hall & Oates fell through when Elektra was substantially reorganised.

The Last Worthless Evening | "The Last Worthless Evening" is a song written by John Corey, Don Henley, and Stan Lynch.  It was a single recorded by Henley in 1989 which reached #21 on US charts.  The song was included on Henley's third album "The End of the Innocence" that same year.
Innocent Chinyoka | Innocent Chinyoka (born 21 June 1982) is a Zimbabwean first-class cricketer who plays for Mashonaland Eagles.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary1

response:
{
  "output": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Passage [1] → "\"The End of the Innocence\" is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album of the same name, released in 1989." → ENTITY: Don Henley; SCOPE: credits Henley as the artist for the single / song release and gives chart performance.
Passage [2] → "The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles." → ENTITY: Don Henley; SCOPE: explicitly identifies Henley's role in (membership of) the Eagles (lead vocalist and drummer).
Passage [3] → "\"Innocent\" is a single by British rock band Stereophonics..." → ENTITY: Stereophonics; SCOPE: unrelated single (not relevant to this question).
